topic: Implications of Sentient AGI on Global Labor Structures

history.text: The introduction of AGI will inevitably lead to massive displacement in the labor market. A universal basic income (UBI) model must be preemptively established. The net utilitarian benefit of automated abundance outweighs the friction of transition.

Universal Basic Income addresses physiological survival but neglects the psychological and deontological value of human agency. Without meaningful labor, we risk widespread existential crisis. We must subsidize cognitive-adaptation rather than passive consumption.

jointDecision.text: Consensus Reached: A phased hybrid approach is optimal. Immediate implementation of a conditional subsistence baseline (UBI-Lite) to mitigate acute survival risks, coupled with mandatory, heavily subsidized cognitive-adaptation training programs (Agency Preservation). This satisfies both immediate utilitarian needs and long-term deontological requirements for human dignity.
